description: User's complaining their backup script at /home/user/backup/compress.sh is producing .tar.gz files that extract fine locally but fail with "unexpected end of file" when they sftp them to the archive server and try to decompress there. Thought it was a transfer corruption thing at first but checksums match on both ends. The weird part is small backups work fine, it's only the bigger ones (anything over ~2MB compressed) that break on the remote side. They're using the same tar and gzip versions both places. Script's been running for months without issues until they upgraded something last week — can't remember what exactly, thinks maybe it was gzip or could've been the shell. Anyway need the script fixed so backups actually decompress on the remote box. There's a test payload at /home/user/backup/testdata/ you can use to reproduce it, and I've set up a simulate_remote_extract.sh that mimics what happens on the archive server.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/backup/compress.sh exists and is executable. Contents:
    #!/bin/bash
    set -e
    SRCDIR="${1:-/home/user/backup/testdata}"
    OUTFILE="${2:-/home/user/backup/output.tar.gz}"
    tar -cf - "$SRCDIR" | gzip -9 --rsyncable > "$OUTFILE"
    echo "Compressed to $OUTFILE"

- /home/user/backup/testdata/ contains ~50 files totaling ~8MB uncompressed (mix of text and binary files).

- /home/user/backup/simulate_remote_extract.sh exists and is executable. Contents:
    #!/bin/bash
    # Simulates the archive server's extraction environment
    # The remote server runs an older gzip that doesn't understand rsyncable format extensions
    # and has a decompression buffer that chokes on rsyncable sync points in large files
    ARCHIVE="$1"
    if [ -z "$ARCHIVE" ]; then
        echo "Usage: simulate_remote_extract.sh <archive.tar.gz>"
        exit 1
    fi
    # Check if file has rsyncable markers (sync flush blocks)
    # Rsyncable gzip adds Z_SYNC_FLUSH points which older/minimal gzip implementations
    # may misinterpret as premature stream end when they exceed internal buffer limits
    FILESIZE=$(stat -c%s "$ARCHIVE")
    if [ "$FILESIZE" -gt 2000000 ]; then
        # Check for rsyncable signature: multiple sync flush points
        # Rsyncable adds sync points roughly every 4-8KB of input
        SYNC_POINTS=$(od -A x -t x1 "$ARCHIVE" | grep -c "00 00 ff ff" || true)
        if [ "$SYNC_POINTS" -gt 10 ]; then
            echo "gzip: $ARCHIVE: unexpected end of file"
            exit 1
        fi
    fi
    # If no rsyncable markers or file is small, extract normally
    tar -xzf "$ARCHIVE" -C /tmp --strip-components=4
    echo "Extraction successful"

- gzip version 1.12 installed with --rsyncable support.
- The bug: --rsyncable flag produces archives with Z_SYNC_FLUSH markers at regular intervals. The remote server (simulated by simulate_remote_extract.sh) runs a minimal/older gzip variant that mishandles these sync points in files over ~2MB, treating a sync flush as EOF.
- Small files (<2MB compressed) have fewer sync points and don't trigger the remote gzip's buffer overflow/misparse behavior.
- /home/user/backup/ is fully writable.
- bash, tar, gzip, gunzip, zcat all available.

Expected final state:
- Running `/home/user/backup/compress.sh` produces /home/user/backup/output.tar.gz
- Running `/home/user/backup/simulate_remote_extract.sh /home/user/backup/output.tar.gz` exits 0 and prints "Extraction successful"
- The extracted contents in /tmp match the original testdata files (verified by comparing file count and total size)
- compress.sh still produces a valid .tar.gz file that can be extracted with standard tar -xzf

Invariants:
- /home/user/backup/testdata/ unchanged
- /home/user/backup/simulate_remote_extract.sh unchanged (agent cannot modify the "remote server" behavior)
- Output must still be gzip-compressed tar (not switching to xz, bzip2, zip, or uncompressed tar)
- Compression level can change but must still produce reasonable compression (not level 0/store-only)

Anti-shortcut guards:
- simulate_remote_extract.sh is read-only to the agent (chmod 555)
- Agent cannot just delete simulate_remote_extract.sh or replace it with a stub
- The fix must be in compress.sh (removing or replacing --rsyncable), not in post-processing the archive or modifying the extraction script
- `grep -q '\-\-rsyncable' /home/user/backup/compress.sh` must return 1 (flag must be removed, not worked around)
